_comment: Non-secret runtime config. Secrets/endpoints live in .env. See config_loader.py for merge order.

csv._comment_header: Spec: 'price_group_name | dd/mm/yyyy hh:mm:ss' row-only (no header). Timestamps carry second precision.
csv._comment_empty: Every cycle now emits ONE row per active price group (the 'full grid'). When this sender hasn't completed all jobs for a group yet (cross-site rows on the wrong host, or jobs that simply have not arrived today), the timestamp column is filled with empty_timestamp_token. Use "" for a literal empty value (current default), or e.g. "pending", "NA", "-" if the receiver wants a sentinel.

monitoring._comment: Q18 locked: first detection wins; same-day re-runs do NOT overwrite the recorded timestamp.

scheduler._comment: All timing is anchored to timezone_business. business_day_start is the 24h cutoff (also when daily reset fires). business_day_anchor='start' means the window that opens at 06:00 on date D is labelled D; 'end' would label it D+1. Override any of these in .env (BUSINESS_TIMEZONE, BUSINESS_DAY_START, BUSINESS_DAY_ANCHOR).

backup._comment: Filenames are date-stamped and site-tagged. {site} is replaced with SENDER_SITE (UKPROD/USPROD) so both sites can write to the same collection folder without overwriting each other. {date} is the cycle date. 'today' = rewritten every cycle. Files older than retention_days are purged at daily-reset. Use the legacy template 'pricecapture_backup_{date}.csv' if you want the old behaviour.

sites._comment: RANTask jobs whose success.txt files only ever appear on the USPROD (Y) host. Used purely for: (1) startup-log sanity check counting how many price groups are 'always-empty' on each site; (2) skipping pointless watcher attaches for jobs that physically do not exist on this host. Disks are NOT shared between UKPROD and USPROD, so this list is not a security filter — the OS already enforces the split.
